title: LiveComponent with send_update parent→child communication
prompt: Write two files: (1) lib/my_app_web/live/dashboard_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.DashboardLive, Phoenix.LiveView). (2) lib/my_app_web/live/dashboard_live/metric_panel.ex (module MyAppWeb.DashboardLive.MetricPanel, Phoenix.LiveComponent). The parent DashboardLive receives a :refresh message via handle_info/2 and must tell each MetricPanel child to refresh via send_update(MetricPanel, id: panel_id, force_refresh: true). The MetricPanel defines update/2 — when assigns.force_refresh is true, it reloads data from MyApp.Metrics.fetch(panel_id) and re-assigns. It also defines mount/1 for initial assigns. Render the panels in the parent with <.live_component module={MetricPanel} id={panel_id} />. NEVER call handle_event in MetricPanel from the parent — all cross-component communication goes through send_update.
tier_rationale: Cross-file LiveComponent coordination requires knowing the send_update contract, update/2 callback semantics, and the architectural rule that LiveComponents don't share state with their parent. Sonnet often tries to message child pids directly or puts state in the parent and re-renders.
